title: malus baccata subsp. toringo
intro: Malus baccata subsp. toringo, also known as the Mongolian crabapple, is a subspecies of the Malus baccata plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malus baccata subsp. toringo include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
